desc: Salento is a colorful Andean town and gateway to Cocora Valley, best for coffee-country scenery, hiking, and cool evenings at moderate altitude.
hls: Access to Cocora Valley wax palm landscapes
Small-town base for coffee farm visits
Pleasant cooler temperatures vs lowland Colombia

todo.name: Cocora Valley full-day hike
todo.desc: Start early for cloud-forest loops and wax palm viewpoints.

todo.name: Coffee finca tour
todo.desc: Join a production tour from bean picking through roasting.

todo.name: Calle Real evening stroll
todo.desc: Explore colorful façades, balconies, and artisan shops.

todo.name: Mirador Alto de la Cruz
todo.desc: Climb for town and mountain vistas at golden hour.

todo.name: Trucha lunch in riverside eateries
todo.desc: Try local trout dishes served with patacones and arepas.

todo.name: Filandia half-day trip
todo.desc: Visit nearby Filandia for quieter coffee-town atmosphere.

wifi.note: Decent in town stays; finca lodges outside town can be inconsistent.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland access usually connects via Bogotá, Medellín, Madrid, or Panama City before a domestic leg; holiday peaks (Dec-Jan, Semana Santa, Jul) increase fare pressure.
prac.currency: Colombia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Mountain weather shifts quickly; trails can become muddy with afternoon rain even in drier months.
Roadworks and landslides occasionally impact transfer times from Armenia/Pereira airports.
Weekend day-trippers crowd Cocora's trailheads—go early or choose weekdays.

sweet: Dec-Mar and Jul-Aug for relatively drier hiking conditions and clearer valley views.
